title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Books and Reading Discussion
theme: Books and reading (links to 'Describe a book that influenced you')
questions: Why do you think some people prefer reading printed books rather than e-books?
How can reading habits differ between younger and older generations?
What role do libraries play in a community today?
Do you think governments should do more to encourage people to read?
In what ways can reading fiction benefit a person's development?
Is it possible that books will one day be completely replaced by digital media?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Role Models and Influence Discussion
theme: Role models and influence (links to 'Describe a person you admire')
questions: What qualities do you think make someone a good role model?
Why are some celebrities considered role models even though people have never met them?
Do you think children today have good role models to look up to?
How much influence do parents have on the values of their children?
Should famous people feel a responsibility to behave well in public?
In what ways can a role model influence someone's career choices?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Travel and Tourism Discussion
theme: Travel and tourism (links to 'Describe a place you would like to visit')
questions: Why do you think international travel has become so popular in recent decades?
What are the benefits of experiencing other cultures first-hand?
How does tourism affect the economy of a country?
What negative impacts can mass tourism have on local communities?
Do you think people will travel more or less in the future?
How might virtual reality change the way people experience new places?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Possessions and Consumerism Discussion
theme: Possessions and consumerism (links to 'Describe a possession that is important to you')
questions: Why do some people place great importance on owning expensive things?
Do you think people today own more possessions than they did in the past?
How does advertising influence what people choose to buy?
What are the downsides of a society that values material possessions?
Do you think people can be happy with very few possessions?
How might attitudes towards ownership change in the future?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Celebrations and Traditions Discussion
theme: Celebrations and traditions (links to 'Describe a memorable celebration')
questions: Why are traditional celebrations important to a society?
How have the ways people celebrate special occasions changed over time?
Do you think globalisation is causing local traditions to disappear?
What is the role of festivals in bringing communities together?
Should traditional celebrations be preserved even if they are expensive?
How might celebrations look different a hundred years from now?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Helping Others and Community Discussion
theme: Helping others and community (links to 'Describe a time you helped someone')
questions: Why do you think some people volunteer their time to help others?
What are the benefits of volunteering, both for individuals and for society?
Do you think people today are less willing to help strangers than in the past?
How can schools encourage young people to help their communities?
Should governments or individuals be more responsible for helping those in need?
In what ways can technology help people support one another?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Hobbies and Leisure Time Discussion
theme: Hobbies and leisure time (links to 'Describe a hobby you enjoy')
questions: Why is it important for people to have hobbies?
How do the hobbies of young people differ from those of older people?
Do you think people have more or less free time than they did in the past?
Should schools give children more time for leisure activities?
How can hobbies contribute to a person's mental health?
Do you think some traditional hobbies are dying out? Why?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Food and Diet Discussion
theme: Food and diet (links to 'Describe a dish you really like')
questions: How have people's eating habits changed over the last few decades?
Why do you think fast food has become so popular around the world?
What can be done to encourage people to eat more healthily?
Do you think traditional home cooking is becoming less common?
How does a country's cuisine reflect its culture?
Should governments tax unhealthy foods to improve public health?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Learning Skills and Education Discussion
theme: Learning skills and education (links to 'Describe a skill you would like to learn')
questions: Why do you think some skills are easier to learn as a child than as an adult?
What is the best way for adults to learn a new skill?
Do you think practical skills are as important as academic knowledge?
How has the internet changed the way people learn new things?
Should schools focus more on teaching life skills?
Will traditional classroom learning still be necessary in the future?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Technology and Society Discussion
theme: Technology and society (links to 'Describe a piece of technology you find useful')
questions: How has technology changed the way people work over the past twenty years?
Do you think people rely too heavily on technology in their daily lives?
What are the risks of children spending too much time using devices?
How might artificial intelligence affect employment in the future?
Should there be more rules about how personal data is used online?
Do the benefits of new technology outweigh the disadvantages?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Music and Culture Discussion
theme: Music and culture (links to 'Describe a song that means a lot to you')
questions: Why do you think music is such an important part of every culture?
How has the way people listen to music changed in recent years?
Do you think traditional music is being lost among younger generations?
What effect can music have on people's emotions and behaviour?
Should music education be a compulsory part of the school curriculum?
How might streaming services be changing the music industry?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Public Spaces and Urban Planning Discussion
theme: Public spaces and urban planning (links to 'Describe a public place you like to visit')
questions: Why are public spaces such as parks important in cities?
How can well-designed public spaces improve people's quality of life?
Do you think modern cities have enough green space?
Who should be responsible for maintaining public spaces?
How can city planning encourage people to spend more time outdoors?
What challenges do growing cities face in providing public spaces?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Decisions and Success Discussion
theme: Decisions and success (links to 'Describe a decision that changed your life')
questions: What factors do people usually consider when making important decisions?
Do you think people make better decisions when they are older?
How much does luck play a part in a person's success?
Is it better to make decisions quickly or to take time to think them over?
How can someone recover from a decision that turns out badly?
Do you think society puts too much pressure on people to be successful?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Friendship and Relationships Discussion
theme: Friendship and relationships (links to 'Describe an old friend')
questions: Why do you think some friendships last a lifetime while others fade?
How has social media changed the way people form and keep friendships?
Do you think online friendships can be as strong as face-to-face ones?
How important are friends compared with family in a person's life?
Do people make friends differently at different stages of life?
What qualities are most important in a close friendship?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Environment and Nature Discussion
theme: Environment and nature (links to 'Describe a natural place you have visited')
questions: Why is it important for people to spend time in natural surroundings?
What are the main threats to natural environments in your country?
How can individuals help to protect the natural world?
Do you think economic development and protecting nature can go together?
Whose responsibility is it to deal with environmental problems?
How might people's relationship with nature change in the future?
